documents.title: Tapestries: vellum-reader bake, GitHub Pages deploy
documents.outcome: Pipeline landed; two publications live. Review the surface and temper if it holds up — see Current State.
documents.body: Current State The static publishing pipeline is built and live. vellum-reader ships bake (Node CLI) + publish subcommand, static-adapter.ts (ReadOnlyAdapter), static-main.tsx + Vite static build target → dist-static/ . Bundled via the existing tsconfig.bake.json alongside the Vite browser build. cailmdaley/tapestries repo migrated: legacy-graph-viewer preserves the prior main ; new main carries the shell ( _vellum/ shared assets, 404.html SPA fallback, per-publication subdirs). GitHub Pages serves from main:/ . Two live publications : https://cailmdaley.github.io/tapestries/constitution-tapestries-publish/ https://cailmdaley.github.io/tapestries/vellum-on-vellum/ Vellum-reader README carries the live vellum-on-vellum screenshot and bake docs. Action for the human: open both URLs, check rendering and search, temper if it holds up. Findings SPA routing on GitHub Pages needs 404.html fallback. No server rewrites available; the 404 page bootstraps the SPA. Standard pattern, but worth recording — every future Pages publication will want this. Bake output → dist/ , matching Vite norms. Considered docs/ (Pages-conventional) but rejected: collides with potential per-repo README/doc folder; dist/ is the cleaner separation. publish subcommand emerged during the work. Originally scoped as “user wraps bake in their own shell”; in practice the deploy shape (git checkout, copy bundle, commit, push) was identical across publications and earned a built-in verb. The “users can still wrap it themselves” property is preserved — publish is a convenience over bake , not a replacement. Open Questions None blocking. The shape is settled; review and temper decides whether anything wants follow-up. Desired State Build the static publishing surface for fibers. vellum-reader bake <fiber> walks a .felt/ store, parses MyST via myst-parser , and emits per-fiber JSON + a Fuse search index + a tiny index.html shell into --out . vellum-reader publish (or a user-built shell) pushes the bundle to a GitHub Pages repo. Vellum-reader becomes reader and producer of its own FiberContent shape; felt grows nothing. Specifics: cailmdaley/tapestries : legacy-graph-viewer branch preserves the prior main . New main carries a fresh shell ( README.md , 404.html , _vellum/ , .gitignore ); per-publication subdirs at /<slug>/ . Pages serves from main:/ . vellum-reader : src/bake/ (Node CLI), src/static-adapter.ts , src/static-main.tsx + Vite static build target → dist-static/ with base: "/tapestries/_vellum/" . package.json adds myst-parser , myst-ext-tabs , myst-ext-proof , js-yaml , fuse.js , katex as runtime deps; React stays peer-dep-only. felt : unchanged. No new verbs, no config. Usage : vellum-reader bake <slug> [--root <path>] [--depth N] [--out <dir>] . Default --root walks up for .felt/ ; --depth defaults to 1 (single fiber, 0 means all descendants); --out defaults to $XDG_CACHE_HOME/vellum-reader/<slug> . URL shape : single-fiber at /tapestries/<bare-slug>/ ; subtree at /tapestries/<root-slug>/<descendant-slug>/ . In-bundle wikilinks navigate internally; out-of-bundle render as stub cards. Bare-slug collisions error at bake time. Done means: bake + publish roundtrip works end-to-end, two live publications serve and render, README screenshot in place, vellum-reader installable via npm install -g github:cailmdaley/vellum-reader . Explicitly out of scope: npm-registry publish, felt publish verb, asset offload (R2/S3), cross-publication wikilink resolution, astra-rendering in the static adapter, round-tripping giscus comments back into fibers. Context This is the publishing surface piece of the broader ai-futures/portolan/vellum-reader/constitution-vellum-extraction arc: vellum-reader was just extracted from the lightcone monorepo, and it needs a way to publish fibers as static sites. The “tapestries” repo is the demo target — it shows what a published fiber surface looks like. Pushing to GitHub Pages is the user’s shell (vellum-reader provides publish , but any user can equally well wrap bake in their own deploy script). This keeps felt out of the publishing business entirely: bake to a directory, let the user push. Felt itself stays 15 MB and zero-config; the producer/consumer symmetry lives in one repo (vellum-reader). The architectural pivot from earlier drafts — bake moved out of felt and into vellum-reader — is recorded in felt history ; the short reason is that the original plan (felt-owned bake with felt publish Go verbs shelling out to TS) changed felt’s character for every user, most of whom never publish.

documents.title: Dogfooding ASF tapestry: friction log
documents.outcome:
Open: annotation panel positioning, body-link nav, annotate→GitHub-issues feature.
Closed in-session: SPA routing fix, asset hash drift, title humanization.
documents.body: Cail’s first real-use case for vellum-reader publish: share the Anthropic STEM Fellowship subtree with his wife for comments. This fiber tracks the friction that surfaced once we ran the publish flow against real content. Closed in this session SPA routing fix. Fresh URLs / shared deep links / refreshes were 404-ing because GitHub Pages serves 404.html for unmatched paths and the existing 404.html referenced a stale bundle hash. publish.ts now regenerates 404.html on every publish via fallbackHtml() in src/bake/html.ts . Asset hash drift across publications. Every publication’s index.html embeds the current reader bundle’s hashed asset paths. When the reader rebuilds, all old publications break unless their shells are regenerated. refreshPublicationShells() in publish.ts rewrites every sibling publication’s index.html on each publish, keeping the whole site consistent against the latest bundle. Title humanization in the root listing. When the root fiber’s name: field is identical to the slug (common in felt), the listing now humanizes the slug ( anthropic-stem-fellowship → “Anthropic Stem Fellowship”) instead of showing the raw slug. Users can override with a non-slug name: value (or a richer pipeline could read a separate title: field later). Open Body-link navigation. Cail reported clicking a fiber link in body prose shows “as a card” but doesn’t navigate. Some of this may have been the SPA routing problem (client-side navigate('/slug') worked but refreshing or sharing the resulting URL 404’d into a broken 404.html ). Worth re-testing after the SPA fix lands. Annotation panel renders on the left, should be right. Margin column positioning derives from --canvas-width (set by CanvasDivider ); if the divider isn’t mounted in the static reader path, the rail computation falls through to an unexpected location. Worth tracing through MarginCitations.measure() (src/components/MarginCitations.tsx ~line 234). Annotate → GitHub issues. The dream: a reader can highlight prose, drop a comment, and the comment auto-creates a GitHub issue against the source fiber’s repo (or a designated comments-repo). Cheap collaborative read-and-mark surface. Big enough to be its own shuttle dispatch — should draft a constitution as a sibling fiber. Use case Sharing ai-futures/application/anthropic-stem-fellowship with Michelle for comments on the Greenhouse drafts before the May 15 deadline. Real day-to-day flow Cail wants to validate the publishing pipeline against.
